description: Per-state animation frame-timing budgets (milliseconds) for tools/timing_lint.py. Encodes the timing doctrine in rules/04-animation.md as machine-checkable bands so the animation rule family gets the same deterministic gate the palette/orphan/silhouette rules already have. Bands are deliberately wide and project-neutral; tighten per project if needed.
matching: A tag is classified into a state when its lowercased name CONTAINS one of the state's `match` keywords (substring). Unmatched tags are skipped (honest > noisy).

fields.per_frame_ms: [min,max] band each frame in the tag must fall within (e.g. idle too fast = jitter, too slow = sluggish).
fields.min_frame_ms: lower bound only, for states whose upper bound is intentionally open (an attack hold is meant to be long).
fields.require_hold_ms: at least one frame in the tag must be held >= this (the impact/hold beat). Pure timing, not the white-flash check (that is combat_lint, Group C).
fields.easing_expected: true => a >=3-frame tag where every frame has identical duration is flagged uniform_timing (no easing).
fields.must_not_loop: true => flag if the tag loops infinitely (repeat == 0); a death/KO state should play once.
